blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: Utilizes a synthetic Synthea cohort rather than real de-identified EHR data.
blocking_gaps_for_10_10.smallest_fix: Partner with a health system to benchmark against a localized, real-world retrospective cohort.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: The in-browser BitNet classifier falls back to a 39-pair pre-computed lookup rather than running full WASM/JS tensor operations for novel pairs.
blocking_gaps_for_10_10.smallest_fix: Compile the BitNet ternary forward pass to WebAssembly to allow true zero-latency client-side classification on arbitrary novel pairs without falling back to Python.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: Tight coupling to the bespoke STARGA 'mind-mem' ecosystem and pending MIC@2/MAP transport protocols.
blocking_gaps_for_10_10.smallest_fix: Provide a standard REST/GraphQL or generic gRPC fallback adapter for the federation layer to maximize interoperability outside the STARGA stack.

strengths: Q16.16 fixed-point BitNet Layer 4.5 achieves bit-identical deterministic execution across all hardware (CPU/GPU/NPU), solving the FDA SaMD reproducibility problem.
Cryptographic federation invariants (PHI gate, X25519, ChaCha20-Poly1305) provide robust, mathematically enforced HIPAA compliance.
Exceptional UI/UX integration utilizing editorial clinical typography, 'trust' palettes, and smooth accessible animations that convey enterprise-grade reliability.
parsed.delta_from_round_3: The catastrophic UI failure from Round 3 has been emphatically resolved. The inlined demo HTML demonstrates an incredibly polished, production-ready interface with sophisticated layout, clinical-grade typography (Newsreader, Fraunces), and interactive 'What-If' simulations. Furthermore, replacing ASCII with Mermaid flowcharts dramatically improves documentation clarity. The visual execution now matches the extreme rigor of the underlying cryptography and deterministic ML layers, moving this from a backend research project to a tier-1 SaMD product.
